relevant_files.role: Main security scanner implementation with regex-based pattern matching
Pattern engine with regex-based vulnerability detection
JSON configuration file with security patterns for regex matching
Integration tests for security scanner including multi-line limitation test
Test fixture with SQL injection vulnerabilities
Test fixture with hardcoded secrets vulnerabilities
Test fixture with safe code patterns (negative tests)

conventions.commits: Conventional commits with GPG signing and DCO sign-off
conventions.testing: Integration tests with test fixtures, AAA pattern, focused test cases
conventions.linting: Rust: cargo clippy -- -D warnings, cargo fmt --check
conventions.error_handling: thiserror for libraries, anyhow for applications, tracing for observability

patterns: Integration tests with vulnerable and safe fixtures
Modular security scanner design with trait-based architecture
SARIF output format for security findings

related_issues.title: feat(security): evaluate tree-sitter for AST-based vulnerability detection
related_issues.relevance: Primary issue proposing tree-sitter integration for multi-line vulnerability detection

related_issues.title: test(security): add negative test documenting multi-line detection limitation
related_issues.relevance: Documents current limitation that tree-sitter would address

related_issues.title: test(security): add negative test for multi-line SQL injection
related_issues.relevance: Merged PR that added test case showing regex limitation

constraints: Must maintain backward compatibility with existing regex patterns
Should not significantly increase build times
Should provide comparable or better performance than regex
Must integrate with existing SARIF output format
test_coverage: Comprehensive integration tests with vulnerable and safe fixtures, but limited to line-by-line detection. Multi-line vulnerabilities are explicitly documented as unsupported.

library_findings.relevant_api: Parser, Tree, Node, Query for AST traversal and pattern matching
library_findings.notes: Core tree-sitter Rust bindings with incremental parsing support
Rust language grammar for tree-sitter, would be first language to integrate

library_findings.relevant_api: Query language for matching patterns in AST
library_findings.notes: Built-in query system for sophisticated pattern matching beyond regex

approaches.name: Hybrid Approach
approaches.description: Combine tree-sitter AST analysis with existing regex patterns. Use tree-sitter for multi-line detection while keeping regex for simple patterns.
pros: Best of both worlds - AST for complex patterns, regex for simple ones
cons: Potential performance overhead from running both engines
Need to maintain two pattern systems

approaches.name: Full AST Replacement
approaches.description: Replace regex patterns entirely with tree-sitter queries. Convert all existing patterns to tree-sitter query format.
pros: Better accuracy for all pattern types
Simpler to extend to new languages
cons: Potential performance impact on simple patterns
Steeper learning curve for pattern authors

approaches.name: Parallel Detection Engine
approaches.description: Run both regex and tree-sitter engines in parallel, merge results. Use tree-sitter only for languages with available grammars.
pros: Can selectively enable tree-sitter per language
Easier to benchmark and compare approaches

recommendation: Start with the Hybrid Approach as it provides the best balance of risk and benefit. It allows for gradual migration, maintains backward compatibility, and addresses the immediate multi-line detection limitation while providing a path to full AST-based detection in the future. The implementation should focus on SQL injection and other multi-line patterns first, then expand to other vulnerability types as the approach proves successful.
